title: Efficient loop filter for video codec
abstract: Presented herein are techniques for filtering pixels during video coding and decoding operations. Similar operations are performed at a video encoder and a video decoder. For a target pixel in a block of a video frame represented by the encoded bit-stream, a value of the target pixel is compared with neighboring pixels to produce a plurality of comparison results. A particular offset value for the target pixel is derived based on the plurality of comparison results. The target pixel is filtered using the particular offset value. This process is performed for some or all of the pixels of blocks of a video frame.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: receiving at a decoder from an encoder an encoded bit-stream representing video frames; for a target pixel in a block of a video frame represented by the encoded bit-stream, comparing a value of the target pixel with neighboring pixels to produce a plurality of comparison results; deriving a particular offset value for the target pixel based on the plurality of comparison results; and filtering the target pixel using the particular offset value. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein deriving the offset value for the target pixel comprises generating an index into stored data of offset values based on the plurality of comparison results, and retrieving the particular offset value from the stored data using the index. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein generating the index comprises generating a multi-bit binary number, each bit of the binary number corresponding to a comparison result between the target pixel and a corresponding one of its neighboring pixels. 4. The method of claim 2, wherein generating the index comprises generating an 8-bit binary number according to the equation: Index = (A > X)*2Â° + (B > X)*2 l + (C > X)*2 2 + (D > X)*2 3 + (E > X)*2 4 + (F > X)*2 5 + (G > X)*2 6 + (H > X)*2 7 , wherein X is value of the target pixel, A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are values for the neighboring pixels at upper diagonal right, above, upper diagonal left, left, right, lower diagonal left, below, and lower diagonal right, respectively, to the target pixel, and "(Y > X)" equals " 1" if the value of pixel Y is greater than the value X of the target pixel and "0" otherwise, where Y is the value of pixels A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H, and wherein the offset values are positive numbers by which the value X of the target pixel may be increased. 5. The method of claim 2, wherein generating the index comprises generating an 8-bit binary number according to the equation: Index = (A < X)*2Â° + (B < X)*2 l + (C < X)*2 2 + (D < X)*2 3 + (E < X)*2 4 + (F < X)*2 5 + (G < X)*2 6 + (H < X)*2 7 , wherein X is the value of the target pixel and A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are values for the adjacent neighboring pixels at upper diagonal right, above, upper diagonal left, left, right, lower diagonal left, below, and lower diagonal right, respectively, to the target pixel,, and "(Y < X)" equals " 1" if the value of pixel Y is less than the value X of the target pixel and "0" otherwise, where Y is the value of pixels A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H, and wherein the offset values are negative offset values by which the value X of the target pixel may be decreased. 6. The method of claim 2, further comprising: storing data at the decoder for a plurality of sets of offset values; receiving at the decoder from the encoder a signal that indicates which particular set of the plurality of sets of offset values to use. 7. The method of claim 2, further comprising: storing data for a single set of offset values consisting of only 0's and l ' s; receiving at the decoder from the encoder a signal indicating a scale factor to be applied to the single set of offset values for use in filtering pixels. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein deriving comprises computing the particular offset value for the target pixel based on a sum of comparison results between a value of the target pixel with a value for each of predetermined neighboring pixels, wherein the comparison result is based on any one of: greater than, less than, equal to, greater than or equal to, less than or equal to, wherein each comparison result is assigned a " 1" or a "0" for purposes of computing the sum. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein deriving the particular offset value for the target pixel comprises performing a computation according to the equation: X = X + ((B > X)+(D > X)+(E > X)+(G > X) > 2) - ((B < X)+(D < X)+(E < X)+(G < X) > 2), wherein X is the value of the target pixel and B, D, E and G are values for the neighboring pixels above, to the left, to the right and below, respectively, the target pixel, wherein (Y > X) equals " 1" if the value of pixel Y is greater than X and "0" otherwise, and (Y < X) equals " 1" if the value of pixel Y 

 is less than X and "0" otherwise, where Y is the value of pixels B, D, E and G, and ((D > X)+(E > X)+(G > X) > 2) equals "1" if ((D > X)+(E > X)+(G > X)) is greater than 2 "0" otherwise, and ((B < X)+(D < X)+(E < X)+(G < X) > 2) equals "1" if ((B < X)+(D < X)+(E < X)+(G < X)) is less than 2 and "0" otherwise, and X is a filtered value of the target pixel. 10. The method of claim 1, and further comprising receiving from the encoder a signal that indicates to the decoder whether to filter at a frame level or at a block level or at both the frame level and the block level. 11. The method of claim 1, further comprising: receiving from the encoder a signal based on an effectiveness of the offset values, wherein filtering is based on the signal received from the encoder. 12. The method of claim 10, wherein the signal sent from the encoder to the decoder is configured to disable the use of the filtering based on the stored data for certain types of block of video frames. 13. A method comprising: at an encoder, for a target pixel in a block of a video frame of a plurality of video frames, comparing a value of the target pixel with neighboring pixels to produce a plurality of comparison results; deriving a particular offset value for the target pixel based on the plurality of comparison results; filtering the target pixel using the particular offset value; and generating an encoded bit-stream based on the filtering. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein deriving the particular offset value for the target pixel comprises generating an index into stored data of offset values based on the plurality of comparison results, and retrieving the particular offset value from the stored data using the index. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein generating the index comprises generating a multi- bit binary number, each bit of the binary number corresponding to a comparison result between the target pixel and a corresponding one of its neighboring pixels. 16. The method of claim 14, further comprising sending a signal sent from the encoder to a decoder indicating to the decoder which particular set of a plurality of sets of offset values the decoder is to use. 17. The method of claim 13, deriving comprises computing the particular offset value for the target pixel based on a sum of comparison results between a value of the target pixel with a value for each of predetermined neighboring pixels, wherein the comparison result is based on any one of: greater than, less than, equal to, greater than or equal to, less than or equal to, wherein each comparison result is assigned a "1" or a "0" for purposes of computing the sum. 18. The method of claim 13, further comprising sending from the encoder to a decoder a signal that indicates to the decoder a scale factor to be applied to offset value for use in filtering pixels. 19. The method of claim 13, further comprising the encoder testing an effectiveness of the filtering, and sending from the encoder to a decoder a signal that is based on the effectiveness of the filtering. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the signal sent from the encoder to the decoder is configured to disable the filtering at the decoder for certain block types based on the effectiveness determined at the encoder. 21. One or more computer readable storage media encoded with software comprising computer executable instructions and when the software is executed operable to perform operations comprising: receiving at a decoder from an encoder an encoded bit-stream representing video frames; for a target pixel in a block of a video frame represented by the encoded bit-stream, comparing a value of the target pixel with neighboring pixels to produce a plurality of comparison results; deriving a particular offset value for the target pixel based on the plurality of comparison results; and filtering the target pixel using the particular offset value. 22. The non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 21, wherein the instructions operable for deriving comprise instructions operable for generating an index into stored data of offset values based on the plurality of comparison results, and retrieving the particular offset value from the stored data using the index. 23. The non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 22, wherein the instructions operable for generating the index comprise instructions for generating a multi-bit binary number, each bit of the binary number corresponding to a comparison result between the target pixel and a corresponding one of its neighboring pixels. 24. The non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 21, wherein the instructions operable for deriving comprise instructions operable for computing the particular offset value for the target pixel based on a sum of comparison results between a value of the target pixel with a value for each of predetermined neighboring pixels, wherein the comparison result is based on any one of: greater than, less than, equal to, greater than or equal to, less than or equal to, wherein each comparison result is assigned a "1" or a "0" for purposes of computing the sum.
